component_identification.content: 1 board map of North America, 240 plastic train cars (45 in each player color plus extras by edition), 110 Train Car cards (12 each of 8 colors plus 14 locomotive wilds), 30 Destination Tickets (short and long routes by edition split), 5 wooden scoring markers, and rules booklet.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn you do exactly one action: draw train cards, claim one route on the map, or draw destination tickets. Claimed routes place your plastic trains on matching colored links and immediately score points. Cards fuel route claims; longer routes need more matching cards but score better. Play proceeds clockwise until an endgame trigger is met.

detailed_rules.content: Route claim: spend the exact number of cards matching route length and color; gray routes accept any one color set. Ferries require locomotive cards shown on route. Tunnels (in Europe variants) are not in base North America and should be ignored unless playing expansion maps. When drawing train cards, you may take two from the deck/open market unless your first pick is a face-up locomotive, which ends your draw at one card. If three locomotives are face-up at once, clear all five face-up cards and refill. Destination tickets are private objectives between two cities; completing grants points, failing loses points at game end. When drawing destination tickets midgame, keep at least one and discard the rest. Route ownership is exclusive in 2-3 player base; double routes are only available in 4-5 player games where both tracks can be independently claimed. You cannot split a route claim across turns. If deck runs out, shuffle discards to form new draw pile.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Points from claimed routes are scored immediately by route length track values (1=1, 2=2, 3=4, 4=7, 5=10, 6=15). At game end, add completed destination ticket values and subtract incomplete ticket values. Award Longest Continuous Path bonus (10 points in base rules) to player(s) with longest uninterrupted network. Endgame starts when any player has 2 or fewer trains left after their turn; every player including trigger gets one final turn, then final scoring decides winner.

beginner_strategy.content: Prioritize connecting overlapping destination tickets so one backbone route satisfies multiple goals. Claim key choke-point links early (for example Nashville–Atlanta or Calgary corridors) before opponents block your plan. Draw cards in pairs that support your next concrete route instead of hoarding random colors. Keep at least one “safe” short ticket completed early so you are never forced into desperate endgame builds. Count opponents’ remaining trains: if ahead, trigger endgame quickly; if behind, avoid dropping to 2 trains until you complete critical tickets.
